summary: Re-review found one functional regression in the new assertion and one remaining coverage gap that still lets the smoke test pass without proving the final `[]` contract.

findings.title: `sawAgentMessage` check looks at the wrong field, so successful runs are reported as failures
findings.why_it_matters: The parser maps Codex agent messages to `event_type: 'status'` with `payload.state: 'agent_message'`, not to `event_type: 'agent_message'`. As written, the new assertion never becomes true on a normal success path, so this smoke test will fail even when the adapter is working.
findings.fix_instructions: Change the loop to detect the parsed agent-message status event shape the adapter actually emits, for example by checking `event.event_type === 'status' && event.payload?.state === 'agent_message'`. Then add/update a test fixture that feeds a real `item.completed` / `agent_message` JSONL chunk through the script logic and proves the flag becomes true.

findings.title: The smoke test still does not verify that the final agent message content is exactly `[]`
findings.why_it_matters: `result.findings.length === 0` only proves the parser produced no findings. It also stays zero when Codex returns a non-JSON or otherwise unparsable final message, so the smoke test can pass without catching a regression in the structured-output contract it claims to validate.
findings.fix_instructions: Capture the text from the final `agent_message` event and assert that its trimmed content is exactly `[]` (or, at minimum, parse it as JSON and assert it is an empty array). Add a regression test covering a plain-English final agent message to ensure the smoke test fails in that case.

fix_plan: Update `scripts/codex-smoke.js` to detect agent messages using the actual event schema (`event_type: 'status'`, `payload.state: 'agent_message'`).
Store the final agent-message text during stream consumption and assert it is exactly an empty JSON array.
Add regression coverage for both cases: a valid `agent_message` JSONL event and an invalid/non-JSON final message that must fail the smoke test.
